This is where it starts getting fun for me. Like literally this is the part, like if you guys were kind of in my office and hanging out, that's what we would do. We'd have a party doing this all day long. It's like my favorite, by far my favorite part of the business. You can ask anyone on my team, we've got whiteboards everywhere and 90% of the time I'm sitting there, what if we do this and we want to move this over here and it's so much fun. So that's what we're going to do. So I'm going to show you guys the nine core funnels that we use. Does not mean there's only nine funnels. There's a bajillion different ways you can do stuff. So there's a lot of different ways to do funnels. I'm just going to show you guys the core ones that we do because that will give you some foundation of how to start building your own off of this and you'll see how we do all these kind of things. So I'm going to go through. So the first funnel is just our core overall funnel. And this is one I showed you guys earlier today but it's basically very simple. This is what it looks like. So I say when I come in here, I'm going to have all the traffic we control, all the paid apps we're going to go to, this squeeze page right here. And from there we're going to sell them something. Then we're going to try to send them up and we're going to try to get them on a continuity. People come from our blog will come through here and then we'll try to sell them, upsell them. So this is kind of like the high level one, right? So it's not very specific but it shows you like a really good overview of kind of what it is. This is why yesterday when we kind of sat down and wanted you guys to look at your value ladder because I want you to have a very good idea of where this top end is going to be, kind of what this front end is going to be, kind of what this is going to be because now we're going to break it down into, this is kind of what's the macro micro, this is the macro and we're going to go into the micro now. That's so much above like my education level to even know that. I wasn't even sure if I was right. So hopefully that's what it actually means. So basically what we're going to be breaking down over these funnels really is just this piece right here, what it could look like, what it should look like. And I promise you as you kind of start seeing these things, a lot of them will probably look familiar to you. Alright, so this one right here, this is as you guys know my favorite. It's the two step free plus shipping funnel. And it's very simple. We take it to a page and the first page is very simple. All I'm basically saying is hey I got this cool thing, where should I ship it to? Okay now tomorrow I'm going to show you guys our script. We have like a video script that we use on this page and the sales letter script I'll show you guys tomorrow. So you know exactly what the verbiage and words and what we use and why we use it. We'll go over all that. But basically it's a page like this. Now here's the free thing I'm selling and step number one I'm going to ask for your shipping and step number two I'm going to come over here and this is where I'm going to ask for your actual credit card information. Okay now there are not a lot of shopping carts out there right now unfortunately they allow you to do two steps like this. So I know a lot of people are like well how do I do that? Work funnels can almost do this. In the very near future this will be something that will be able to do two steps like this where step one you ask for their shipping and step number two you ask for their credit card. Now a couple interesting things about this type of funnel that we found the first time we started kind of testing and playing with it we saw this a lot happening in like the CPA world. So if you guys know that it's where a lot of like free trial based stuff and people are making crazy amounts of money with it. It's kind of not scammy but it's kind of like the edge of what people do in marketing. I was looking at these guys' pages and the page was like this. You come here and it's like all these fields like where do you want to ship the free trial to? I'm like no one's ever going to fill out like 10 different fields and I was like why in the world it just didn't make any sense to me right? And so I wanted to see kind of what happened so we would test that we would drive a little bit of traffic to see what would happen and for whatever reason they worked really really good. And then we would sit down and figure out why does that work? Why does that work? And I think and I'm not sure because we've also tried it where like we asked for all of their credit card information here and it's never really converted and I think that it's this whole like slippery slope thing we talked about right? You're going to take this first commitment and then the next and it gets easier and easier and so the first one's just like hey where do you want me to ship it to? And then at that point a lot of people I don't know if they don't know or they don't, I'm not sure why but they just fill in the credit card really quick or their address like ship it here and they click it and the next step is like okay how do you want to pay for shipping and there's a drop off between step one and step two but the conversion rates that we're experiencing on this first one right here are almost as high as like a squeeze page. And what's interesting is that it's like a glorified squeeze page for me. I'm getting their entire shipping address which we haven't been using but you could and then they're coming here where we get their credit card and so it's just kind of a cool blend of like a squeeze page and a sales page into one doing a two step like that. So it's really kind of a cool thing. And one other thing that we found out when we were doing our free plus shipping with our supplement is there's companies you can hire for people that fill out page one, step one and not step two and they'll just call all those people and they'll say hey you were here but you didn't buy it, what can you do? And they'll sell people on step two for you and they get really, really high conversion rates on that so that's kind of another cool thing about this funnel is that all the unconverted leads that don't quite finish the process, there are call centers you can get to go and help push people through. Alright, now after they've gone through this, again here they qualify themselves as a subscriber, here they qualify themselves as a buyer, now I want to find out who my hyperactors are, right? This is where I start getting into the upsells, okay? If you remember yesterday we talked about the value ladder, right? This is where we start pulling out some of this value ladder stuff. So if you guys pull back out your value ladder and you remember what you put for the different levels, this is where we start introducing all that stuff, right? So here's your value ladder. So we've just taken somebody through the bottom of this value ladder, they've just paid for the free push and they've received a little bit of value and now we're going to start sending them up to the next thing. You know, typically right here I'm not going to sell my $25,000 coaching program because I haven't gotten enough value yet, right? So I've got to think the psychology behind it, like they just bought this thing about how to lose weight, okay? Or they just bought this credit card knife or they just bought this, where's my MLM one, maybe it's gone. Anyway, they just bought this thing about how to sell high ticket products. I just took them through this process where they got a piece of something, right? I want to kind of stress this again because I've had a couple of questions about this, but remember yesterday I was talking about the one thing? What I found over the last couple of months, like if this thing is like tied to one thing, you know, like the one secret about how I do this, or the one trick or the one really cool thing, this works the best because the next thing you want to sell, if you're selling more of the exact same thing, it just doesn't convert well, doesn't sell well for whatever reason. The first time I kind of tested that, those of you guys will talk about the black box funnel, but we did this black box funnel where we give away the dot com secret slabs that were upselling them in invisible funnel about more testing and conversion stuff and it bombed, it was horrible. And I was trying to think, why is it bombed? And I think it's because they bought this thing on conversion and I tried to sell more thing on conversion. It doesn't, it's just, it doesn't, they don't want more of the same thing. It does not usually convert well, but it does work really well, is a couple of things. One of them, one of them is what is the next thing? Okay? So if you look at our current dot com secret slabs funnel right now, the one that's converting, that's killing it for us, the book is conversions, right? So my next, my first upsell is like, hey, I gave you everything you needed to ever know about conversions, but what good are conversions if you don't want people to actually show up to your site? The next thing you need, if you're going to be part of our team, is you've got to have traffic coming to your site. So what we've got now is this really cool product to teach you how to get traffic. Okay? It's the next thing that they need in the funnel. That makes sense? And then the third, and then we have another upsell, and then the third upsell is like, you got traffic, you've got, you've got, you've got conversions. The third thing you need in this funnel is you've got to have, you've got to know how to sell. And so here's our products and teach you how to sell. So we went from, we went from conversion to traffic to sell. So the next thing is, is a big thing in your upsell flow, right? So what's the next thing they need? The next one, I learned this from Ryan Dice, I think it's really cool. He calls it a bucket offer. Okay? So if you're upsell for a bucket offer, basically what a bucket offer is, where they kind of, excuse me, started doing this, they would do these events. This is probably four, four or five years ago when they first started doing them. They would do an event like this, and they would, they would record each module as a, as a thing, and then they made each module a front end product. And so you would buy it. For example, one of the very, very first ones they did was, was how to make money with, with Facebook ads. This was right when Facebook was coming out and doing their stuff, and they sold this front end product, and they crushed it with it. So you buy this front end product, but how to make, buy ads on Facebook? And the upsell was, hey, just so you know, that, that presentation you just purchased was one of eight presentations we did in an event, and we wanted to let you have access to the entire thing. So you got a piece we want to give you the whole. Okay? So they call that a bucket offer. Okay? They, they got one scoop of the bucket out, and that's what you sold them, and now you're going to sell them the rest of, of everything else. Okay? So you're thinking about your front end. A lot of times it's just, like we talked about with your value ladder. You're taking something big over here, and you're pulling out the one thing, the best thing, the most sexy thing, and that becomes your front end. Okay? So the next, the bucket, and then the other thing that works, that, that works really good is, like, faster and better. So you just bought this thing right here, and let me show you how to do it faster and better. Okay? That works really good too. So those are kind of the, the core three things I'm trying to do for an upsell. So when you think about your value ladder, again, you've got down here the one thing that, that attracted and brought them in. Now what's, what's either the next step, the bucket of everything, or how can you help them do that faster and better? Usually faster and better offers are like software. Like, hey, I just gave you this thing to teach you one thing. Get this offer and help you do it faster and better. Right? So that's kind of the third one there. That make sense you guys? So you got to start thinking about your product, and start thinking like, after somebody buys my front end, what's, what's the next logical upsell? What should this be? Okay? Now, one thing I'll kind of show you just because you'll see this common theme throughout all these different funnels is you have an upsell. So let's say someone pays, you know, 495 shipping handling here. Then you got an upsell. There's different schools that thought my personal belief pattern is I like sending people up in price like this. So I go from 495 to then I increase it, and then I increase it, and I go bigger every single upsell. So if they notice something, then you can drop and do a downsell, which I lower the price. Okay? Now, I'm sure the next question people can ask me is like, what price should I sell the upsell for? And I can't answer that because every market is going to be so different. What we sell to our weight loss clients is way different than our internet marketing clients. Internet marketing clients will pay $300 on how to get traffic. Weight loss clients won't pay $300 to learn how to lose weight faster. Right? They might be able to, but it's a lot harder. So I can't give you a pricing thing, but it's usually bigger. I would say on average you're looking about a 10 times increase. So I'm selling this product that's $10 right here. I'm going to sell $97 product on the next thing. If it's $5, I'm going to sell for about $50. Something about 10 times is usually where I go for my first step. Do you have a question? Great question. So the question was on the downsell, do you discount the offer or do you do a two-pay, three-pay? What's your conversation with, right here today, he was talking about some guys, and I think this is shady and I hate it and I don't think you should do it. They have their offer and then if you say no, they're like, okay, I'll give you the same thing for $10 cheaper. You say, no, okay, $20 cheaper. No, okay, $30. And he was dropping the price. And I think that's horrible to do to your customers. It loses all your credibility and faith. It's just a bad thing. So for downsells, there's usually a couple of different ways that I do it. One way is to drop down to a payment plan. So let's say it says $97. I come down and I say, look, my page here would say something like, if you couldn't afford it, it's the only logical reason why, you can't afford it right now. So I'm going to do a two-pay. So I'm going to do two payments of $47. So a payment plan, basically. So a payment plan downsell works really, really well. The other thing I like to do is a lot of times I'll shift how we fulfill it. So for example, let's say this $97 thing is like a physical product I'm going to ship you. And I'll say, okay, well, if you can't afford that, how about this? We'll do it cheaper, but it will just give you the digital version instead. So shifting how they consume the content works really, really good. And the other one is just changing the offer. Like, hey, it looks like you're interested in that, but maybe you're interested in this instead. Okay? So again, I'm going here. I'm sending them up. They say no, then I drop them down to a lower price point or to a payment plan. And then from here, you can end it. Or if you have a second product, then you come back and you can upsell them a second product. They say no, you can downsell. And some people can go like this forever. If you're an embarrassment, you end up 30 days later still clicking SNOs and keep taking these things. Not typically a good idea. When I first got started, I was really aggressive when I got started. I was like, I want to make as much money as possible. So I'd have, we always have like upsell, downsell, upsell, downsell, upsell, downsell. So we've said no to everything. It was six no's. You said yes to everything, you saw three. And we should always do that. And our customer experience, the first time I think I ever noticed it, is when you guys gave a shopper approved and they're like, hey, there's this really cool thing. You put it on your thank you page and your customer's review, like how great their experience was buying with you. I'm like, oh, cool. That'd be awesome. See how good it is. He's like, yeah, we put it on all these big sites and everybody let you, like everyone gets four and five stars and because they just bought your thing, they love you right now. I'm like, okay, cool. So we put it on. And do you remember this? Like the first day he shows me the thing and we have like all two stars. Like everyone's pissed. I'm like, take that thing off. Like turn, take it off now. I don't want to see that. And he's like, no, you got to read the reviews. I'm like, I don't, like, it hurts, right? And I'm like, crap, it's her reading. Everyone's like, I'm so excited about the product. I'm like, 20th upsell, I wanted to kill you. And like all these things. And I was just like, huh. If my goal is to, is to give people value and to send them up and someday have them pay me a lot of money to hang out with me, I got to figure out where the line is and I want to make sure not to cross it, right? And so we went from doing six to typically we'll do just one in a downsell or maybe two. And that's kind of where we max out at. It's up to you and your comfort level what you want to do. But again, if your long-term vision is taking somebody somewhere cool, sometimes you got to give up short-term profits for long-term profits. So it's a question to do a refund policy in the front end. They're all free plus shipping. We do, we say, like in the copy, it's like, we'll give you refund even if you're five bucks, even if you're shipping, we'll refund. And you keep the DVD, whatever, you tell them that. But very few people ever ask for refund on a free plus shipping thing. If you look at the copy on our page, you'll see at the bottom how we do the risk reversal because it is in there. We basically say, keep the DVD and we'll give you your five bucks back shipping handling. Okay, so does that kind of make sense, upsells, downsells? You're going to use these in a lot of these different funnels. So I wanted you to kind of get some of the core concepts behind those with this front end offer. Now, you can do, what's cool about all these offers, you guys, is kind of like Legos, right? You can like, I'm giving you tons of different blocks and you can move them around. So you might do upsells downsells, like Russell, I hate free plus shipping, you pull it out and you're going to plop in one of these other building blocks. I'm just giving you guys a building block and showing you a couple of ways that we do it. So it'll help you when you're kind of building your own. Yes. So this question was, should you test like two front ends at the beginning and then see when it wins or should you just do one? Probably like the answer probably should be two, but I don't do that. I like doing one just to like, I always try to make my best, my best foot for, make this as close where I think it's going to be best and I do it. If it doesn't work, then I'll come back and try to figure out what, what, but I don't want twice as much effort initially. I'd rather test like headlines and little things like that that are easier as opposed to making two whole front end products. Now if the front end product doesn't work, then I'll go make a new front end product really quick, but I'm not going to redo the whole thing. That's the other really cool thing about it, you guys, is after you get this thing working, right, and you start driving traffic, it's working, it's working really good, you can then take a new front end, a new free DVD or a new whatever and keep the same upsell, downsells. These upsells, downsells are converting. You can plug those into anything. A lot of our, a lot of our business, you guys, is just re-skidding these little front ends. Everything back here is all the same. What's interesting is that a lot of people will buy all the upsells multiple times. It's really weird. I had some of their data that they bought one of the upsells and they refunded and they emailed, they're like, man, that sell switch was good. It's the third time I bought it. Because my thing is I'm trying to, I'm trying to get new blood in, right? So a new hook gets them in the door, then we can set them up the ladder. I'm always trying to find out what's the new hook I can do to get somebody in the door, get some in the door. And, and everyone, because like, for example, you may be really interested in like microcontinuity, like that, like when I talk about that, that's really appealing to you. And I talk about high ticket stuff, you're like bored. But you over here may be like, high ticket stuff is like the great, so I talk about that, you're in, right? So I throw a lot of different bait to attract different people. And after I get them in, usually most of this back end stuff is very similar, if not the same, because I'm just trying to attract as many people into my funnel, the types of people I want. So there's, there's our free plus shipping two step funnel. All right. The next funnel here is a tripwire funnel. This is one that Perry and Ryan have made famous over the last six months or so. And, and I think it's really cool the way that they do it. So this is, I think they have five steps in there, and this illustrates four of the five different things. So it was interesting about them that their business model is kind of trying to get everybody to fit into this mold, which I think is a good mold. I think works for 90% of the businesses, but it is a funnel, one of many different types of funnel. What they teach, and I think it's great. So the front end, they have what they call a lead magnet, which is their squeeze page. They're giving away a free something, right? Boom. Then they up, as soon as you get the free thing, they upsell you on what they call a tripwire, which is typically a super low ticket product. A lot of theirs are like $7 offers or free plus shipping. And if they're the upsell, the core offer, which is the actual product you want to sell, which is your middle of the value ladder type product. They downsell you on a payment plan for that product, and then after that they try to profit maximize, which is puts you into their high ticket thing. I'm going to show you guys some of the funnels we use to get people in the high ticket thing here in a second, but that's kind of the way that their funnel works. So I know that Ken's still in the room. Ken worked for Ryan and Perry for about six months or so, and this is what we did every single client that came in and said, okay, boom, here's the five steps. What's your free plus shipping? Boom. What's your tripwire? We do that every single day with hundreds of clients. They go through that and build it out. This funnel works great. It's awesome. That's what that funnel looks like. What they do a lot of times is they'll do the same concept of this one thing, but they're like, what's the one thing? And they'll have you write like a 15 page report on that one thing, and they sell out for $7. And again, there's benefits to that. I told you guys early why I don't like doing that. I like the word free. I want to use free as much as I can to get the right people in. But most of their offers in the internet marketing space, if you notice, are all $7 offers. All their products in the physical space are all free plus shipping. So it's the same concept, but it's your low end, front of the value ladder offer that you're bringing somebody through. One thing when they were first explained that was cool is that they said, your core offers that most people are typically selling are, this is the core thing I'm selling. So what they did is they take your core thing and you just like take one piece out of it and make that the tripwire. And then same thing we talked about, the 100 visitor test. You get way more people by your core offer because they already said yes to this very first thing. I agree 110% with everything they say on that. So that's kind of how they do theirs. All right. Next is the microcontinuity funnel. Anyone here remember microcontinuity? I hope you guys. This was a, we're going to be relaunching this product here in the near future, but this was a funnel we built, man, when was it? It was a little while ago. And we made a ton of money with it. And we showed a bunch of people and I can't tell you how many people have told me over and over again the very first time I ever made it was microcontinuity. And so this is a microcontinuity funnel. You can use the same model with like a membership site as well. But the problem I've always had with membership sites, anyone here have a membership site? The hardest problem with them is that like, you're kind of like giving yourself a death sentence. Like when you start a membership site, you can never stop ever, right? It sucks. We've had like four or five membership sites. We always shut them down eventually because it's like every month they want new content and new stuff and new stuff and new stuff and every month you're doing more stuff and more stuff. And then a year later, somebody comes in, he logs in and there's like a billion things in there and he's just overwhelmed and they just leave because they're confused too. And it's like these things, I'm just not a huge fan of membership sites. I like them for the recurring money, but not as much. So what we started doing is these microcontinuity sites where they were finite. So it was like a program, right? Kind of like you go to school and it's 18 weeks you're going to learn this topic, right? And every week you're going to go to class and at the end of it you'll get a degree and you'll be done. So we started doing a lot of our training this way where we say, okay, come in, we're going to teach you this topic and week number one, you're going to log in members there and you'll get this thing. And then seven days later we'll unlock the next week and you'll get this thing. And then seven days later you'll get this and every week you get more training and handouts. And eventually at the end of it, it's over and you graduate and you stop paying. What was cool is that for a customer it worked really, really well as well because a lot of times customers come in and they're joining this thing and it's $40 a month for forever. And there's like this weird thing where it's like, ah, and so the stand for a month or two and then they cancel. The average, excuse me, the average stick rate for membership site in the internet marketing, weight loss in most industries, guess how long it is? The average stick rate. Under three months. That crazy? So an average for maybe two, two and a half months. You shift it to this style, also they know it's $37 a month for six months. Now there's the end date and there's a progress, there's a point, there's a reason why they're on this thing. They're not logging in and getting a million ideas, they're getting one thing and they have to do that and the next week they get the next thing. Because of that your stick rate will, I mean our majority of our people would stay, not the majority of our people, but our drop off rate was a fraction of what it was for typical membership site. Huge percentage of people would go all the way to the end, all six months or five months whatever it is because they want to complete that thing that they're going towards. It's a goal, there's a reason. So that's why I love microcontinue membership sites. What we would do, this is the way we did this funnel and it worked awesome for people, it's super easy. The very first page we have our squeeze page and what I would do on the squeeze page is it comes back to that one thing again. I wanted everyone to find one secret that they had. One of our best examples was Joy Anderson, she lives here in Idaho and she made a microcontinuity site about how to start your own preschool from home. So I said what's the number one secret you have? So I can't remember her secret, something like how to make money without cleaning out poop all day or something around poop. I remember it was really funny. The first thing was like I have a free video that's going to reveal to you the number one secret about how to do whatever. Our headline we always use for us and all of our students was free video, free video reveals number one secret to fill in the blank. Free video reveals number one secret to losing weight, free video reveals number one secret to increasing your income, to whatever your secret is, getting better grades in school to do whatever. So someone would come and they would give you their email address. Then on the next page there would be a video and this video would now take some of the value out. You told me you were going to give them a free secret. Right here they would give you the free secret. It takes them up one rung up the value ladder. So my free secret is this. They would actually give that person the secret. A good example like my potato gun offer. I think I told you every time we have a new funnel we try out, we always test it with our potato gun one. So for this one on my landing page it says free video reveals the number one secret to shooting a potato 50 yards further than your friends. In the video I would be there like hey this is my name is Russell and I have a secret that is going to show you exactly how to shoot a potato 50 yards further than your friends. My number one secret is most people think that the type of propellant used in your potato guns the best one is Aquanet hairspray. What we found is that right guard under armed deodorant is actually more flammable than your potato would shoot farther just by switching from Aquanet to right guard. Which by the way as a new fact no longer is true. Right guard pulled the propellant out and no longer works in potato guns. Did anyone know that? You guys impressed that I knew that? Anyway. So no longer is my secret true but at the time that was the secret right? Right guard was the big secret. And so everyone goes and what do they have? So that was the script we always use for microcontinuity and then from there we'd sign them up for what we do is we get free DVD with five other secrets plus I'm going to let you be part of my online coaching program. awesome even in markets you don't think it would work one of our businesses in the couponing market which by the way is the worst market to get into because nobody wants to pay things just the nature of the customer they don't want to spend money right and so we kept trying all these things nobody would buy anything we're like there's got to be a way to sell these guys something um and the invisible funnel worked we made an invisible funnel where um our our couponing girl named jessica hacker she comes in and basically says i'm going to show you guys how to um how to coupon and it's free you're gonna come in you're gonna go through this program and at the end of it i'm gonna show you how to save uh like 100 dollars on your grocery bill and if i and then uh basically you're gonna learn how to save 100 dollars in grocery bill and then she comes back and says if you don't lose save 100 dollars in your grocery bill then let me know i won't bill you anything but if you do then i'm just gonna bill you 20 dollars okay so i'm gonna basically give you 100 dollars in exchange for 20 dollars and uh it's worked great um we also did this in the speed reading market one of my friends is sat down and he sat there and he read her entire book in what five six minutes read her whole book and the man he started quizzing him and he knew the answer to every single thing she said it's the coolest thing you will ever see and so when he hadn't come to our office i was like i want you to teach me and everybody else how to how to increase the reading so we took our entire company through the process and uh i think the worst person in the room doubled their reading in four hours most people were five six seven times as fast it was the coolest thing in the world so we created an invisible funnel and this is this thing about the invisible funnel you get the right hook it these things just blow up like crazy so our hook for him was like hey it's called double your reading dot com and it was like we howard burr will teach you how to double your reading in three hours or you pay nothing so come on the thing put your credit card and we'll charge you a penny you show up you go through this process you take a test at the beginning at the end if you haven't doubled your reading then we'll give your money back if you have doubled it don't do anything we'll bill you 97 bucks i say this nicely he'll probably someday watch this video i feel bad he is not a good-looking guy by any stretch of the imagination right so the big hook is like look at me i'm overweight not very
